rootCause.label: GPT prepass prefers fraction metadata (1/8, 1/2) over visible Qtd decimals

rejects.A_cropLacksQtd: Qtd strips show 1,35 / 1,83 / 4,30; Prosciutto correct on same crop
rejects.D_parsingBug: 2.00 first appears at GPT JSON output

rootCause.downstreamImpact: Integer prepass fails isFractionalQty → anchorQuantities scope gate skip → Gorgonzola stays Pass C 1.05

requiredQuestion.rationale: Integer prepass=2 clusters on Gorgonzola (1/8) and Bresaola (1/2); Prosciutto without fraction reads correctly; Mortadella has fraction but prepass≈PDF; Pellegrino x15 is separate metadata class

finalVerdict.rationale: Column strip removes description from vision input (fixes root cause); fraction-conflict heuristic widens ocr_qty_mismatch when scope gate would miss bad integer OCR

anchorQuantitiesInput.scopeGate: isQtyAnchorScopeRow — fractional kg + Emporio discount semantics
anchorQuantitiesInput.blindSpot: integer ocr_quantity skips scope → ocr_qty_mismatch hardcoded false

A_promptOnly.evidence: v39 prompt already lists 1/8 as NOT purchased qty; live prepass returns 2

B_deterministicQtdOnly.gap: scope gate still blind to residual bad integer if strip fails

C_promptPlusValidation.risk: does not supply correct ocr_quantity for anchoring

E_other.tesseractOnQtdStrip: defer — tuning burden; GPT on strip sufficient for v1

task3_deterministicQtdDesign.principle: Read ONLY Qtd column pixels; ignore Designação; return null if unreadable
task3_deterministicQtdDesign.approach: vertical Qtd strip crop after table crop, before runQuantityPrePass
task3_deterministicQtdDesign.rowAlignment: index-order matching; name optional in strip mode (row-0, row-1, …)
failOpen: strip crop fails → full table crop + hardened prompt (T4)
prepass null per row → skip anchor for that row; retain Pass C qty

task4_promptHardening.when: fallback only — full table crop when strip crop unavailable
minimalAdditions: The image may show ONLY the quantity column — no product names are visible.
Each horizontal band is one row's Qtd cell. Read top-to-bottom in table order.
Pack fractions in product names (1/8, 1/2, 1/4) are NEVER purchased quantity.
If a cell is blank or illegible → quantity: null (never infer 1, 2, or pack count).
Integer 2 is almost never a valid Emporio kg Qtd when the cell shows a decimal like 1,35.
task4_promptHardening.stripModeUserMessage: Read the decimal quantity from each row band in this Qtd column image only. Return one item per visible row band, in order. Name may be row-N.
task4_promptHardening.note: Do not deploy prompt-only; existing v39 rules already violated

currentBehavior.scopeLimit: flag only set inside isQtyAnchorScopeRow branches
currentBehavior.gorgonzolaGap: prepass=2 → scope false → ocr_qty_mismatch false at 47.5% delta

proposedAdjunct.name: fractionDescriptionConflict
proposedAdjunct.condition: name matches /\d+\s*\/\s*\d+/ AND prepass.quantity is integer AND passC.quantity is fractional kg AND deltaPct > 10%
proposedAdjunct.action: set ocr_qty_mismatch true in metadata (detection only)
proposedAdjunct.reason: flags for review; never auto-overwrites qty

validationAlone.reason: cannot supply correct ocr_quantity for anchorQuantities; user still gets wrong persisted qty

afterDeterministicQtd.existingOcrQtyMismatch: retained as safety net for S3-style disagreements

expectedAfter.note: strip may fix; else heuristic flag
control — different supplier layout; no change

task7_beforeAfterMatrix.assumptions: Deterministic Qtd strip + existing v39 anchoring on Emporio May 2026 re-extract

files.change: strip mode prompt; fraction-conflict meta helper
pass bounds to prepass; log strip vs fallback
